evals.prompt: I'm writing a paper on a new drug delivery system. I have data showing: (1) nanoparticle characterization (size, zeta potential across 4 formulations), (2) in vitro release curves over 72 hours, (3) cell viability at 3 concentrations. I need to design Figure 1 for Nature Materials. Can you plan the figure layout?
evals.expected_output: A figure plan with panel layout, evidence hierarchy, archetype selection, and panel-to-claim mapping
expectations: Plans panel layout with evidence hierarchy (hero + supporting)
Maps each panel to a unique claim
Generates figure description file after planning

evals.prompt: I have a CSV with treatment groups (Control, Drug_A, Drug_B, Drug_C), each with n=8 biological replicates, measuring tumor volume over time. I want to show the treatment effect. Nature submission.
evals.expected_output: Profile data, select appropriate chart, intercept if needed, generate figure + description
expectations: Profiles data to check n per group
Selects appropriate chart (line + error band for time series)
Does NOT intercept (n=8 is acceptable for the selected chart type)
Exports PDF + PNG with Nature specs
Generates fig_description.md with core conclusion, stats, findings

evals.prompt: I'm preparing a manuscript on CRISPR gene editing efficiency. I need 4 figures: Figure 1 is the experimental design schematic + validation, Figure 2 is editing efficiency comparison across 5 guide RNAs, Figure 3 is off-target analysis, Figure 4 is functional outcomes. Plan all four figures for Science.
evals.expected_output: Complete 4-figure plan with cross-figure consistency, narrative flow, and per-figure descriptions
expectations: Plans 4 figures with distinct core conclusions
Establishes visual vocabulary in Figure 1 and reuses across figures
Panel ordering follows narrative logic (system → effect → mechanism → robustness)
Specifies shared elements (colors, legends) across figures
Each figure has archetype, panel map, evidence hierarchy
